description: Mapping of SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria (CC controls) to AWS Config managed rule names.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Identification and Authentication
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — New User Registration
controls.description: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Role-Based Access
controls.description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access based on roles and responsibilities.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Access Restriction
controls.description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Credential Rotation
controls.description: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or modify information is removed.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Boundary Protection
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Data Transmission
controls.description: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users.

controls.control_name: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Malicious Software Prevention
controls.description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software.
To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations.
The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts.
The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives.
The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure components.
The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities.
The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.
The entity manages risks associated with storing sensitive data in database systems.
The entity manages rotation of credentials stored in secrets management systems.
